title: Surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom
abstract: A surgical instrument (100) with multiple degrees of freedom, said instrument (100) comprises: at least a head portion (1, 2) comprising at least a distal tool head (1); at least a body portion (4, 5), said body portion (4, 5) being spaced apart from said head portion (1, 2), said spaced apart region providing the ability to provide for multiple degrees of freedom of said at least a head portion (1, 2); and at least a central system (3) configured to connect said at least a head portion (1, 2) to said at least a body portion (4, 5) and further configured to achieve maneuverability and locking ability to said at least a head portion (1, 2) in multiple degrees of freedom.
claims:
Claims Claims, 1. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom, said instrument comprising: - at least a head portion comprising at least a distal tool head; - at least a body portion, said body portion being spaced apart from said head portion, said spaced apart region providing the ability to provide for multiple degrees of freedom of said at least a head portion; and - at least a central system configured to connect said at least a head portion to said at least a body portion and further configured to achieve maneuverability and locking ability to said at least a head portion in multiple degrees of freedom, characterised in that, said central system further comprising: - an assembly of shafts; - at least a rotator mechanism configured to angularly displace said at least a head portion; - ... at least a universal joint configured to translate angular displacement of said at least a rotator mechanism to said at least head portion irrespective of its transversely angularly displacement position with respect to said at least a body portion; - at least a crank mechanism configured to communicably couple said assembly of shafts with said at least a head portion; and - at least a collar with a pre-defmed slot at operative distal end of at least a shaft from said assembly of shafts so that as said at least a shaft moves operatively upward and downward, it causes transverse angular displacement of said at least a head portion. 2. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said assembly of shafts comprising at least a first inner shaft, at least a second middle shaft co-axial and outer to said at least a first inner 

 shaft, and at least a third outer shaft co-axial and outer to said at least a second middle shaft. 3. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said assembly of shafts comprising at least a first inner shaft, at least a second middle shaft co-axial and outer to said at least a first inner shaft, and at least a third outer shaft co-axial and outer to said at least a second middle shaft and wherein said at least a crank assembly being configured to communicably couple said at least a second middle shaft with said at least a head portion. 4. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said assembly of shafts comprising at least a first inner shaft, at least a second middle shaft co-axial and outer to said at least a first inner shaft, and at least a third outer shaft co-axial and outer to said at least a , second, middle shaft and wherein said at least a collar with a pre-defined slot at operative distal end of said at least a second middle shaft so that as said at least a second middle shaft moves operatively upward and downward, it causes transverse angular displacement of said at least a head portion 5. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said instrument comprising: - at least a first inner shaft configured to provide actuation of said tool head; - at least a first trigger configured to provided actuation of said tool head, - at least a second middle shaft configured to provide a first set of degrees of freedom for said at least a head portion, said at least a second middle shaft being co-axial to and inside said at least a first inner shaft, said at 

 least a second middle ' shaft' being configured to provide angular displacement to said at least a head portion; - at least a third outer shaft configured to provide support for actuating assemblies or motion translating mechanisms from said at least a first inner shaft and from said at least a second middle shaft, said at least a third outer shaft being co-axial to and inside said at least a second inner shaft, said at least a third outer shaft being connected to said at least a head portion by means of a hinged link which allows for transverse angular displacement of said at least a head portion with respect to said at least a body portion restricting its medial - lateral displacement; and - at least a second trigger configured such that its actuation motion is translated in to an operative forward and rearward motion of said at least a second middle shaft. 6. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said instrument comprising at least a first trigger configured to provide actuation of said tool head, said at least a first trigger being a handle with a stationary part and a moving part. 7. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said instrument comprising at least a first trigger configured to provide actuation of said tool head, said at least a first trigger being an angularly displaceable knob with at least a cam which translates an angular displacement motion to a motion desired by said at least a tool head. 8. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said instrument comprising at least a first trigger configured to provide actuation of said tool head, said at least a first trigger being a push button for an on - off function. 9. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said rotator mechanism being communicably coupled to said at least a first inner shaft, further wherein, said at least a rotator mechanism being angularly displaced, about its axis, the communicably coupled first inner shaft angularly displaces about its axis, and therefore, said at least a distal tool head angularly displaces about its axis. 10. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said at least a universal joint being provided at the operative distal end of said at least a body portion, and ensconced with said at least a third outer shaft, such that, it translates angular displacement of said at least a rotator mechanism to said at least a head portion irrespective of its transversely angularly displacement position with respect to said at least a body portion. 1 1.A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said at least a crank mechanism is a series of links starting from the said at least a second middle shaft and hinged to said at least a head portion 12. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said instrument comprising at least a second trigger configured to provide actuation of said at least a second middle shaft, said at least a second trigger being a stub with a cam, which stub is angularly displaceable, and which stub is disposed in a cantilever manner from said at least a second middle shaft. 13. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 

 1 wherein, said instrument comprising at least a second trigger configured to provide actuation of said at least a second middle shaft, said at least a second trigger being provided with at least a locking mechanism. 14. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said at least a pre-defined slot of said collar is a linear operatively transverse slot such that said at least a second middle shaft moves operatively upward and downward at its one end which is located inside said slot. 15. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said at least a central system comprising at least a back rotating collar connected to at least a front rotating collar via said at least a universal joint. 16. A .surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said at least a central system comprising at least a slider-crank system consisting of at least a rigid cylindrical rod and, at least a rigid slider sliding on at least a cylinder, said at least a cylinder being connected to said at least a central rod which is disposed within said at least a back rotating collar. 17. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said at least a central system comprising two rigid links. 18. A surgical instrument with multiple degrees of freedom, said tool head being selected from a group of tools consisting of a grasper, a pair of scissors, a stapler, a scope, a drill, a tissue retractor, a cauterizing mechanism, a sterilizing mechanism, and such instruments.
